text.title: Migrations and CD Blockers
text.summary: Expand-contract migration pattern for safe schema changes, and concrete solutions to the three most common CD blockers: large batches, slow tests, and non-backward-compatible database changes.

rule.statement: Use the expand-contract pattern for schema changes: Deploy 1 adds nullable column (backward compatible). Deploy 2 backfills data. Deploy 3 makes column required. Never combine expand and contract in one deployment.
rule.rationale: Combining schema and application changes in one deploy creates a window where the old application version is incompatible with the new schema, requiring a coordinated cutover that defeats CD.

rule.statement: Require two separate PRs for expand and contract phases; agents that merge them into one PR must be blocked by review policy.
rule.rationale: The single-PR "shortcut" is the most common way CD-adopting teams reintroduce risky coordinated deployments.

example.note: Each deploy can be rolled back independently. Old application versions continue working during the transition.

rule.statement: Target <200 LOC per PR; large PRs are the #1 CD blocker. Implement PR size linting in CI (fail on >500 LOC changes outside generated files).
rule.rationale: Large PRs take longer to review, accumulate more conflicts, and carry more deployment risk than small ones. The discipline must be enforced, not just recommended.

rule.statement: Parallelize tests; run E2E only on staging, not on every branch. Use contract testing instead of full E2E for service boundaries.
rule.rationale: Serial test execution and full E2E on every commit are the two most common reasons pipelines exceed the 15-minute budget and teams start batching.

rule.statement: Fix or quarantine flaky tests immediately; a flaky test in the critical path destroys confidence in CD and encourages manual overrides.
rule.rationale: Once engineers learn to ignore a red pipeline step, they stop treating it as a gate — the entire CD discipline erodes.

antipattern.description: Agents copy "Phase X: Y weeks" timelines from planning documents into roadmaps.
antipattern.reason: Project policy prohibits time estimates in SDD documents. Replace with complexity tiers (Low/Medium/High) and token estimates.

antipattern.description: Using the in-process feature flag cache from the README in production (in-process dict cache).
antipattern.reason: An in-process cache hides flag changes for hours or until the process restarts. Use an SSE/WebSocket-based flag SDK or a very short TTL (under 60 seconds) in production.
